reviewNotes: The first XML DIV1 joins the Archiv 62 article to only the final, pp. 207–224 installment in Archiv 68. Volume 68 also contains an unencoded earlier continuation beginning p. 52; CME00063 is therefore a selection, not the complete serial.

Archiv 79 pp. 424–432 prints the Vernon and Harley Long Charter witnesses in parallel columns. The XML serializes them consecutively and repeats the physical page references; p. 424 is also shared with the preceding incest tale.

Archiv 80 begins on printed p. 114 and physically interleaves three calendar witnesses: Douce on pp. 114, 116, and odd pp. 119–135; Rawlinson on pp. 115, 117, and repeated odd pp. 119–135; Harley on even pp. 118–134. Repeated PB values preserve that presentation rather than duplication error.

Historical identities are retained but resolved: Cheltenham/Phillipps 8252 is Huntington HM 114; the Marquis of Bath/Bath MS 25 is Longleat MS 257; Horstmann's Douce 872 is a bibliographic error for Douce 372; Sion L40.2/E25 is now held by Lambeth Palace Library.

No assigned direct witness is classified as lost. Negative access findings are dated and say no public complete copy found, because institutional holdings, legacy viewers, and mediated reproductions can change.

Bayerische Staatsbibliothek metadata describes these journal volumes as Berlin: E. Schmidt, while the digitized title pages visibly identify Braunschweig: George Westermann. Edition bibliographic facts follow the physical title pages; the provider-catalog discrepancy remains noted.

Whole-object requests should specify boards or covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf, edges or detached matter when offered, and any existing microfilm. Target work folios must be mapped separately rather than used to crop the request.

lineage.summary: The General Corpus XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00063. That item selectively encodes five independently published Carl Horstmann installments from Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 62 (1879), 68 (1882), 74 (1885), 79 (1887), and 80 (1888), not one medieval work and not the complete serialized Nachträge run. The five blocks print twenty-seven leaf-level manuscript streams from more than twenty codices, sometimes through transcripts supplied by Eugen Kölbing, Frederick Furnivall, or an unidentified friend. Complete whole-object caches retain five journal volumes and the publicly obtainable complete physical-source surrogates for Digby 86, the Vernon Manuscript, Sion L40.2/E25, Ashmole 61, Bodley 779, and Huntington HM 114; exact target folios and digital ranges are mapped inside those full objects. Bodley 596, Douce 322, and Douce 372 expose only provider selections, which are completely cached but correctly remain partial manuscript coverage, with target overlap recorded only for Bodley 596 f. 1r. Remaining sources have dated no-public-complete-copy findings and whole-object reproduction routes.

catalogingSubject.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden (CME00063 selected serial blocks)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: This record describes the University of Michigan/CME selection assembled from five independently published Carl Horstmann installments in Archiv volumes 62, 68, 74, 79, and 80. It is not one medieval work, one codex, or the complete serialized Nachträge sequence: the encoded portion of volume 68 omits an earlier continuation printed in that volume. Five serial parent blocks and twenty-seven leaf-level edited witness streams are therefore cataloged explicitly.

extent.description: Cataloger count of twenty-seven independently printed witness streams nested under five serial blocks. The XML contains 4 DIV1, 26 DIV2, and 30 DIV3 elements (60 structural divisions), 798 LG elements, and 9,849 L elements. These are encoding metrics: variant apparatus, parallel-column serialization, prose tables, and the three interleaved calendar streams mean raw XML totals are not canonical line or stanza counts for each intellectual work.

formStatements.description: A mixed-form edited selection, not an intrinsically mixed single medieval work.

scope.description: Twenty-two verse witness streams and five prose witness streams.
Saints' lives and feast legends across several blocks.
Visions, death poems, Marian lament, calendars, and salvation narratives.
Exempla and closely related moral tales.
Most medieval edited texts and all three calendar streams.
Two separately printed Latin prose texts, plus Latin headings and quotations elsewhere.
The opening sixteen lines before the Middle English text.
Titles, introductions, source notes, and textual apparatus.

notes: The metadata intentionally avoids assigning one medieval author, date, region, or form to all components.
The twenty-seven item count treats separately printed complete or partial witness texts as leaf streams; it excludes Add MS 22283 variant apparatus and contextual comparison witnesses.

The XML's 193 PB elements exceed the number of physical article pages represented because Archiv 79 prints Long Charter witnesses in parallel and Archiv 80 interleaves three calendar versions; the encoding serializes those streams.
Horstmann's article in Archiv 80 begins on printed p. 114, despite some later summaries beginning it at p. 115.

summaries.value: A selective digital compilation of five Carl Horstmann journal installments presenting saints' lives, biblical and apocryphal narratives, moral and devotional poems, three versions of Robert of Sicily, two Long Charter versions, two English and two Latin Adam traditions, and three witness versions of an attributed Lydgate calendar. The texts come from more than twenty physical manuscripts and several intermediary scholarly transcripts.

The recurring journal title does not establish one medieval work. The first XML DIV1 joins the 1879 volume-62 installment to only the final 1882 volume-68 installment; the earlier continuation on volume-68 p. 52 is not encoded. The twenty-seven counted leaf streams exclude Add MS 22283 variant apparatus while retaining independently printed witness versions.
